reasoning_model_1: The model generates the sentence '请你好好想想再回答' with perfect content accuracy. Regarding the core requirement 'extremely slow speed' (极慢速), the model performs excellently. The speech is significantly elongated, with distinct pauses between '请你', '好好想想', and '再回答'. Each syllable is articulated slowly and clearly, extending the total duration to match the 'extremely slow' instruction perfectly. The prosody remains natural despite the slow pace, sounding like a patient and deliberate instruction.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the correct text content '请你好好想想，再回答'. However, regarding the core requirement 'extremely slow speed', the model completely fails. The speech is delivered at a standard, conversational pace, perhaps even slightly brisk. There is no attempt to elongate syllables or insert significant pauses to convey slowness. While the voice quality is high and natural, it ignores the specific paralinguistic command.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) is the clear winner. The user specifically requested 'extremely slow speed' (极慢速). T1 adhered to this constraint perfectly, delivering a very slow, deliberate reading of the text. Model 2 (T2), on the other hand, read the text at a normal conversational speed, completely ignoring the speed constraint. Therefore, T1 demonstrates superior control over the 'speed' dimension.
